files.notes: This file contains a single complete short story by Lillith Shell about an orphaned boy who seeks a mother figure. The story ends with the caption about planting the first 'Mothers' Tree' which appears to be related content but part of the same article section.

splits.author: Thomas L. Martin
splits.start_boundary: Intellectual Requirements of "Mormonism" Thomas L. Martin—Professor of
splits.end_boundary: most intellectually active people on earth. If we do

files.notes: The file contains three distinct articles. The first is a complete short story 'The Triumph of Love' by Sylvia. The second is about garden flowers for Decoration Day by Philena Fletcher Homer. The third appears to be cut off mid-sentence and discusses intellectual requirements of Mormonism by Thomas L. Martin. The visual boundaries are clear with title formatting and author bylines separating each article.

splits.start_boundary: Relief Society Annual Report For the Year 1925 Amy
splits.end_boundary: Total Disbursements and Bal- ances on Hand $448,597.85

files.notes: The file contains two distinct sections: the main 'Notes from the Field' article by Amy Brown Lyman covering summer work programs and organizational changes, followed by the 'Relief Society Annual Report' which is a financial and statistical report for 1925. Both are administrative/organizational content that would fall under Notes_from_Field category.
